name: Ouster TOP
description: License: Only available in TouchDesigner Educational, TouchDesigner Commercial and TouchDesigner Pro.
summary: License: Only available in TouchDesigner Educational, TouchDesigner Commercial and TouchDesigner Pro.

parameters.name: Active
parameters.label: Active
parameters.description: Enables connections with the device.

parameters.name: Device Address
parameters.label: Device Address
parameters.description: deviceaddress - The IP address or the name of the Ouster device. The address is only required during configuraton. The device will request an address from the local DHCP server when it is connected to the network. The name of the device is printed on the top of the sensor in the format "os-#####", where ##### is the serial number e.g. "os-991900123456". You can determine the IP address using the ping command and the device name e.g. ping -4 os-991900123456. For more information see the Ouster User Guide at Ouster.io.

parameters.name: Lidar Port
parameters.label: Lidar Port
parameters.description: lidarport - The UDP port number to receive lidar data.

parameters.name: IMU Port
parameters.label: IMU Port
parameters.description: imuport - The UDP port number to receive data from the inertial measurement unit (IMU) on the device. The IMU data can be accessed by connecting the Ouster  to an Info CHOP.

parameters.name: Command Port
parameters.label: Command Port
parameters.description: commandport - The TCP/IP port number to use to send configuration commands to the device.

parameters.name: Target Address
parameters.label: Target Address
parameters.description: targetaddress - The IP address where the sensor should send the lidar and IMU data to. If the parameter is blank, the address of the current machine will be used. This field should only be necessary if the sending machine has more than one IP address or if you wish to send the lidar data to a different machine than the one you are configuring it on.

parameters.name: Local Address
parameters.label: Local Address
parameters.description: localaddress - An IP address for the current machine that should be used to connect to the device with. If the address is left blank, the default network address will be used.

parameters.name: Scan Mode
parameters.label: Scan Mode
parameters.description:
scanmode -  - Select a scanning mode to set the sensor's horizontal resolution and number of revolutions per second. The vertical resolution is determined by the hardware e.g. an OS1-64 sensor has vertical resolution of 64 pixels (samples).

 mode512x10 - mode512x20 - mode1024x10 - mode1024x20 - mode2048x10 -

parameters.name: 512 x 10Hz
parameters.label: 512 x 10Hz
parameters.description: mode512x10 - mode512x20 - mode1024x10 - mode1024x20 - mode2048x10 -

parameters.name: Configure Device
parameters.label: Configure Device
parameters.description: configdevice - Enable this toggle to have the Ouster  set the configuration properties of the device. If the device has already been configured to the correct mode and network connections than this can be disabled to save some processing time.

parameters.name: Image Layout
parameters.label: Image Layout
parameters.description:
layout -  - Use this parameter to determine how data is arranged in the output image. The layout of data is generally not important when used as a point cloud.

 pointcloud - Sensor data is arranged chronologically in a square texture according to when it was received by the scanner. If there are fewer points than pixels in the image, the remaining pixels are filled with the floating point value NaN. image - In panoramic mode, the samples are arranged to form a continuous picture of the area around the sensor.

parameters.name: Scan Order
parameters.label: Scan Order
parameters.description: pointcloud - Sensor data is arranged chronologically in a square texture according to when it was received by the scanner. If there are fewer points than pixels in the image, the remaining pixels are filled with the floating point value NaN. image - In panoramic mode, the samples are arranged to form a continuous picture of the area around the sensor.

parameters.name: Red
parameters.label: Red
parameters.description:
redchannel -  - Select what sensor data will be placed into the red channel of the output image.

 x - y - z - range - intensity - reflectivity - noise -

parameters.name: X
parameters.label: X
parameters.description: x - y - z - range - intensity - reflectivity - noise -

parameters.name: Green
parameters.label: Green
parameters.description:
greenchannel -  - Select what sensor data will be placed into the green channel of the output image.

 x - y - z - range - intensity - reflectivity - noise -

parameters.name: Blue
parameters.label: Blue
parameters.description:
bluechannel -  - Select what sensor data will be placed into the blue channel of the output image.

 x - y - z - range - intensity - reflectivity - noise -

parameters.name: Alpha
parameters.label: Alpha
parameters.description:
alphachannel -  - Select what sensor data will be placed into the alpha channel of the output image.

 x - y - z - range - intensity - reflectivity - noise -

parameters.name: Time Sync Mode
parameters.label: Time Sync Mode
parameters.description:
timemode -  - Select how the sensor generates timestamp information.

 internalosc - Timestamps are generated from an internal oscillator. This is the default setting. syncpulsein - Timing is synced to pulses on the SYNC_PULSE_IN input. ptp1588 - Timing is synced to an external PTP master.

parameters.name: Internal OSC
parameters.label: Internal OSC
parameters.description: internalosc - Timestamps are generated from an internal oscillator. This is the default setting. syncpulsein - Timing is synced to pulses on the SYNC_PULSE_IN input. ptp1588 - Timing is synced to an external PTP master.

parameters.name: Sync Pulse In Polarity
parameters.label: Sync Pulse In Polarity
parameters.description:
pulseinpolarity -  - The polarity of the SYNC_PULSE_IN signal to use.

 activelow - activehigh -

parameters.name: Active Low
parameters.label: Active Low
parameters.description: activelow - activehigh -

parameters.name: Multipurpose IO Mode
parameters.label: Multipurpose IO Mode
parameters.description:
iomode -  - Determines how the sensor uses the SYNC_PULSE_OUT signal.

 off - The signal is not used. This is the default setting. inputnmea - The sensor will expect standard NMEA $GPRMC UART messages on the multipurpose IO port. See here for more information on GPS NMEA data. outputinternalosc - Signal output is taken from the internal oscillator. outputsyncpulsein - outputptp1588 - outputangle - Signal output is based on the angle of the encoder.

parameters.name: Off
parameters.label: Off
parameters.description: off - The signal is not used. This is the default setting. inputnmea - The sensor will expect standard NMEA $GPRMC UART messages on the multipurpose IO port. See here for more information on GPS NMEA data. outputinternalosc - Signal output is taken from the internal oscillator. outputsyncpulsein - outputptp1588 - outputangle - Signal output is based on the angle of the encoder.

parameters.name: Sync Pulse Out Polarity
parameters.label: Sync Pulse Out Polarity
parameters.description:
pulseoutpolarity -  - Polarity of the output signal pulse.

 activelow - activehigh -

parameters.name: Sync Pulse Out Frequency
parameters.label: Sync Pulse Out Frequency
parameters.description: pulseoutfrequency - Frequency of the output pulse in Hz (must be greater than 0).

parameters.name: Sync Pulse Out Angle
parameters.label: Sync Pulse Out Angle
parameters.description: pulseoutangle - The encoder angle at which to output a signal pulse. Measured in degrees less than 360.

parameters.name: Sync Pulse Out Width
parameters.label: Sync Pulse Out Width
parameters.description: pulseoutwidth - Width of the output signal pulse in mm.

parameters.name: NMEA In Polarity
parameters.label: NMEA In Polarity
parameters.description:
nmeainpolarity -  - Sets the polarity of the NMEA URT input $GPRMC messages. Set to 'Active High' if UART is active high, idle low, and the start bit is after a falling edge.

 activelow - activehigh -

parameters.name: NMEA Ignore Valid Char
parameters.label: NMEA Ignore Valid Char
parameters.description: nmeaignorevalidchar - Turn off, if the NMEA UART input $GPRMC messages should be ignored if valid character is not set, and turn on if messages should be used for time syncing regardless of the valid character.

parameters.name: NMEA Baud Rate
parameters.label: NMEA Baud Rate
parameters.description:
nmeabaudrate -  - The baud rate for the incoming NMEA URT input $GPRMC messages.

 baud9600 - baud115200 -

parameters.name: Baud 9600
parameters.label: Baud 9600
parameters.description: baud9600 - baud115200 -

parameters.name: NMEA Leap Seconds
parameters.label: NMEA Leap Seconds
parameters.description: nmealeapseconds - An integer number of leap seconds that will be added to the UDP timestamp when calculating seconds since 00:00:00 Thursday, 1 Jan 1970. Set to 0 for Unix Epoch Time.

parameters.name: Auto Start
parameters.label: Auto Start
parameters.description: autostart - Tell the sensor to automatically begin sending data when it turns on. The default is On.

parameters.name: Output Resolution
parameters.label: Output Resolution
parameters.description:
outputresolution -  - quickly change the resolution of the 's data.

 useinput - Uses the input's resolution. eighth - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. quarter - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. half - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 2x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 4x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 8x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. fit - Grow or shrink the input resolution to fit this resolution, while keeping the aspect ratio the same. limit - Limit the input resolution to be not larger than this resolution, while keeping the aspect ratio the same. custom - Directly control the width and height.

parameters.name: Use Input
parameters.label: Use Input
parameters.description: useinput - Uses the input's resolution. eighth - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. quarter - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. half - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 2x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 4x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 8x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. fit - Grow or shrink the input resolution to fit this resolution, while keeping the aspect ratio the same. limit - Limit the input resolution to be not larger than this resolution, while keeping the aspect ratio the same. custom - Directly control the width and height.

parameters.name: Resolution
parameters.label: Resolution
parameters.description:
resolution -  - Enabled only when the  parameter is set to Custom . Some Generators like Constant and Ramp do not use inputs and only use this field to determine their size. The drop down menu on the right provides some commonly used resolutions.

 resolutionw - resolutionh -

parameters.name: W
parameters.label: W
parameters.description: resolutionw - resolutionh -

parameters.name: Resolution Menu
parameters.label: Resolution Menu
parameters.description: resmenu - A drop-down menu with some commonly used resolutions.

parameters.name: Use Global Res Multiplier
parameters.label: Use Global Res Multiplier
parameters.description: resmult - Uses the Global  Multiplier found in Edit>Preferences>TOPs. This multiplies all the TOPs resolutions by the set amount. This is handy when working on computers with different hardware specifications. If a project is designed on a desktop workstation with lots of graphics memory, a user on a laptop with only 64MB VRAM can set the Global  Multiplier to a value of half or quarter so it runs at an acceptable speed. By checking this checkbox on, this  is affected by the global multiplier.

parameters.name: Output Aspect
parameters.label: Output Aspect
parameters.description:
outputaspect -  - Sets the image aspect ratio allowing any textures to be viewed in any size. Watch for unexpected results when compositing TOPs with different aspect ratios. (You can define images with non-square pixels using xres, yres, aspectx, aspecty where xres/yres != aspectx/aspecty.)

 useinput - Uses the input's aspect ratio. resolution - Uses the aspect of the image's defined resolution (ie 512x256 would be 2:1), whereby each pixel is square. custom - Lets you explicitly define a custom aspect ratio in the Aspect parameter below.

parameters.name: Aspect
parameters.label: Aspect
parameters.description:
aspect -  - Use when Output Aspect parameter is set to Custom Aspect.

 aspect1 - aspect2 -

parameters.name: Aspect1
parameters.label: Aspect1
parameters.description: aspect1 - aspect2 -

parameters.name: Aspect Menu
parameters.label: Aspect Menu
parameters.description: armenu - A drop-down menu with some commonly used aspect ratios.

parameters.name: Input Smoothness
parameters.label: Input Smoothness
parameters.description:
inputfiltertype -  - This controls pixel filtering on the input image of the .

 nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. This is how you get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail.

parameters.name: Nearest Pixel
parameters.label: Nearest Pixel
parameters.description: nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. This is how you get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail.

parameters.name: Fill Viewer
parameters.label: Fill Viewer
parameters.description:
fillmode -  - Determine how the  image is displayed in the viewer.
NOTE:To get an understanding of how TOPs work with images, you will want to set this to Native  as you lay down TOPs when starting out. This will let you see what is actually happening without any automatic viewer resizing.


 useinput - Uses the same Fill Viewer settings as it's input. fill - Stretches the image to fit the edges of the viewer. width - Stretches image to fit viewer horizontally. height - Stretches image to fit viewer vertically. best - Stretches or squashes image so no part of image is cropped. outside - Stretches or squashes image so image fills viewer while constraining it's proportions. This often leads to part of image getting cropped by viewer. nativeres - Displays the native resolution of the image in the viewer.

parameters.name: Viewer Smoothness
parameters.label: Viewer Smoothness
parameters.description:
filtertype -  - This controls pixel filtering in the viewers.

 nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. Use this to get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail. When the input is 32-bit float format, only nearest filtering will be used (regardless of what is selected).

parameters.name: Passes
parameters.label: Passes
parameters.description: npasses - Duplicates the operation of the  the specified number of times. For every pass after the first it takes the result of the previous pass and replaces the node's first input with the result of the previous pass. One exception to this is the GLSL TOP when using compute shaders, where the input will continue to be the connected 's image.

parameters.name: Channel Mask
parameters.label: Channel Mask
parameters.description: chanmask - Allows you to choose which channels (R, G, B, or A) the  will operate on. All channels are selected by default.

parameters.name: Pixel Format
parameters.label: Pixel Format
parameters.description: Pixel Format parameter.
